content:
type: DEFER
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
spec: Add a new module-level variable named hash_sha256 in outlines/types/__init__.py, placed after the existing isbn Regex definition (near the end of the file). Definition: hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'). This must be an instance of the Regex class from outlines.types.dsl (already imported at top of file). No other imports are needed. Behavior required: hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855') == True (accepts exactly 64 hex chars, any case mix); rejects strings that are shorter (63 chars) or longer (65 chars) than 64, strings containing non-hex characters (g-z, special chars, spaces), empty strings, and strings with leading/trailing whitespace. Since Regex.matches uses fullmatch semantics via the DSL, the raw pattern [a-fA-F0-9]{64} is sufficient (no need for ^/$ anchors) as long as Term.validate/matches uses fullmatch -- please verify against how isbn/email types validate in this same file so behavior is consistent with other Regex types. hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__ should yield {'type': 'string', 'pattern': '[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'} inherited automatically from Regex base class, no extra code needed.
note: hash_sha256 feature - single line addition, no other files touched

Behavior required: hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855') == True (accepts exactly 64 hex chars, any case mix); rejects strings that are shorter (63 chars) or longer (65 chars) than 64, strings containing non-hex characters (g-z, special chars, spaces), empty strings, and strings with leading/trailing whitespace. Since Regex.matches uses fullmatch semantics via the DSL, the raw pattern [a-fA-F0-9]{64} is sufficient (no need for ^/$ anchors) as long as Term.validate/matches uses fullmatch -- please verify against how isbn/email types validate in this same file so behavior is consistent with other Regex types. hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__ should yield {'type': 'string', 'pattern': '[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'} inherited automatically from Regex base class, no extra code needed.
